categories.name: Network Infrastructure
categories.description: Core networking components including firewalls, switches, wireless, and cabling for a secure, HIPAA-compliant practice network.

decisions.title: Network Architecture & Design
decisions.description: Choose the overall network topology and management approach for your practice.

options.description: Full-stack Meraki (switches, APs, firewall) with cloud dashboard for unified management.
Ubiquiti UniFi ecosystem with self-hosted controller for switches, APs, and gateway.
Cisco Catalyst or HPE Aruba switches with separate firewall and wireless from best-of-breed vendors.
Outsource entire network design and management to a healthcare-focused MSP.

decisions.title: Firewall & Security Appliance
decisions.description: Select a firewall to protect PHI and segment your clinical network.

options.description: FortiGate 40F/60F next-gen firewall with UTM, IPS, and SSL inspection.
Cloud-managed security appliance with auto VPN, content filtering, and threat protection.
Open-source firewall on commodity hardware or Netgate appliance.
SonicWall TZ270/370 with TotalSecure bundle for SMB practices.

decisions.title: Wireless Network System
decisions.description: Deploy reliable, secure Wi-Fi for clinical devices, staff, and optional patient access.

options.description: Cloud-managed Wi-Fi 6/6E access points with built-in RADIUS and guest portal.
Ubiquiti UniFi U6 Pro/Enterprise APs with self-hosted controller.
HPE Aruba SMB-focused cloud-managed APs with simple setup.

decisions.title: Structured Cabling & Infrastructure
decisions.description: Plan and install network cabling, patch panels, and rack infrastructure.

options.description: Certified Cat6A cabling with labeled patch panels, cable management, and testing.
Standard Cat6 cabling sufficient for 1Gbps throughout the practice.
Minimize cable runs; use Wi-Fi 6E for most devices with cabling only for APs and critical devices.

decisions.title: UPS & Power Protection
decisions.description: Protect critical IT equipment from power outages and surges.

options.description: APC Smart-UPS 1500VA/2200VA line-interactive UPS for server room and network closet.
CyberPower OR/PR series with LCD management and SNMP monitoring.
Quality surge protectors for all IT equipment without battery backup.

decisions.title: Internet Service
decisions.description: Select primary and backup internet connectivity for the practice.

options.description: Dedicated business fiber (500Mbps+) with 4G/5G cellular failover.
Business-class cable internet with DSL line as backup.
Software-defined WAN bonding two ISP connections for performance and redundancy.
Single business-class internet connection without redundancy.

decisions.title: Server Infrastructure
decisions.description: Decide on on-premises server needs for local applications, file storage, and services.

options.description: Small-form-factor server for Active Directory, file shares, print server, and local backups.
Dell T-series tower server with redundant power and RAID storage.
Use cloud-hosted EHR and SaaS for everything; no local server needed.
Synology or QNAP NAS for file storage and local backup without a full server.

categories.name: Endpoints & Peripherals
categories.description: Workstations, mobile devices, printers, scanners, and medical input devices for clinical and front-office staff.

decisions.title: Clinical Workstations
decisions.description: Select computers for exam rooms, nursing stations, and provider offices.

options.description: Ultra-small form factor desktops mounted behind monitors in exam rooms.
HP mini desktops with healthcare-grade reliability and manageability.
HP t640/Dell Wyse thin clients connecting to virtual desktops.
Cybernet or Tangent medical all-in-one PCs with antimicrobial housing and hot-swappable batteries.

decisions.title: Front Office Computers
decisions.description: Workstations for reception, scheduling, and administrative staff.

options.description: Dell OptiPlex or HP ProDesk SFF desktops with dual monitor support.
Dell Latitude or HP EliteBook laptops with USB-C docking stations for flexibility.
Chrome OS devices for practices using fully cloud-based EHR and SaaS tools.

decisions.title: Mobile Devices
decisions.description: Tablets and smartphones for providers doing rounds or point-of-care documentation.

options.description: Apple iPads managed with Jamf or Mosyle MDM for clinical use.
Samsung tablets with Knox security platform for HIPAA-compliant mobile access.
Allow providers to use personal devices with workspace containerization (Microsoft Intune).
Use only fixed workstations; no mobile device deployment.

decisions.title: Printers & Scanners
decisions.description: Multifunction printers, label printers, and document scanners for clinical and admin use.

options.description: HP enterprise multifunction laser printer with secure pull-print and scan-to-email.
Lease MFPs with per-page pricing including toner, maintenance, and support.
Cost-effective Brother laser printers with Fujitsu ScanSnap document scanners.

decisions.title: Medical Input Devices
decisions.description: Specialized input devices such as barcode scanners, signature pads, and dictation microphones.

options.description: Honeywell barcode scanners, Topaz signature pads, and Philips SpeechMike dictation mics per workstation.
Honeywell or Zebra USB barcode scanners for medication/specimen scanning.
Nuance Dragon Medical One with PowerMic for voice-driven EHR documentation.

categories.name: EHR & Practice Management
categories.description: Electronic Health Records, practice management, patient portal, e-prescribing, and clinical decision support systems.

decisions.title: EHR System
decisions.description: Select the core Electronic Health Record system for clinical documentation, orders, and patient management.

options.description: Cloud-based EHR with integrated RCM services and nationwide network insights.
Comprehensive EHR/PM with telehealth, patient engagement, and population health tools.
Host through a regional health system on their Epic instance for interoperability.
Specialty-focused EHR with strong ambulatory workflow and reporting.
Open-source EHR with practice management, e-prescribing, and patient portal.

decisions.title: Practice Management System
decisions.description: Scheduling, billing, claims, and revenue cycle management platform.

options.description: Use the PM module built into your chosen EHR system.
Cloud-based PM with billing, scheduling, and patient engagement for independent practices.
Cloud PM suite with revenue cycle, scheduling, and patient engagement tools.

decisions.title: Patient Portal
decisions.description: Online portal for patients to view records, message providers, schedule, and pay bills.

options.description: Use the patient portal included with your EHR (e.g., MyChart, athenaPatient, eCW Patient Portal).
Third-party portal with self-scheduling, intake, payments, and telehealth.
Comprehensive patient engagement platform with portal, intake, surveys, and campaigns.

decisions.title: E-Prescribing (EPCS)
decisions.description: Electronic prescribing including controlled substances (EPCS) compliance.

options.description: Use Surescripts e-prescribing built into your EHR with EPCS add-on.
Standalone e-prescribing with EPCS, medication management, and clinical alerts.
Embeddable e-prescribing API for integration into custom or specialized workflows.

decisions.title: Clinical Decision Support
decisions.description: Alerts, reminders, and evidence-based recommendations at the point of care.

options.description: Leverage CDS rules and alerts built into your EHR system.
AI-powered diagnostic clinical decision support with image-based differential diagnosis.
Embed UpToDate clinical reference within EHR workflows for evidence-based guidance.

decisions.title: Lab Interfaces & Orders
decisions.description: Electronic lab ordering, results delivery, and reference lab interfaces.

options.description: Direct HL7 interfaces with national reference labs through your EHR.
API-based lab network connecting to 50,000+ labs with FHIR integration.
Fax-based lab orders and manual result entry into EHR.

decisions.title: Document Management
decisions.description: Scanning, indexing, storage, and retrieval of clinical and administrative documents.

options.description: Use document scanning and management features within your EHR.
Enterprise content management with healthcare-specific workflows and EHR integration.
Cloud document management with intelligent indexing and workflow automation.

categories.name: Clinical Devices
categories.description: Connected medical devices for ECG, vitals, point-of-care testing, spirometry, imaging, and more.

decisions.title: ECG/EKG System
decisions.description: Electrocardiogram device selection with EHR integration for cardiac screening.

options.description: 12-lead resting ECG with direct EHR integration and cloud review.
12-lead ECG with touchscreen, Wi-Fi, and PDF export at a competitive price.
Portable 6-lead ECG for basic cardiac screening via smartphone.
Refer all ECGs to cardiology or diagnostic center.

decisions.title: Vitals Monitoring System
decisions.description: Connected vital signs monitors for automated vitals capture into EHR.

options.description: Connected spot-check vital signs monitor with direct EHR integration.
Compact vital signs monitor with BP, SpO2, and temp at a competitive price.
Traditional standalone BP cuff, pulse ox, thermometer with manual EHR entry.

decisions.title: Point-of-Care Testing (POCT)
decisions.description: In-office lab testing devices for rapid results.

options.description: Handheld blood analyzer for comprehensive POCT panels (chemistry, blood gas, cardiac markers).
Standard CLIA-waived testing: rapid strep, flu, COVID, urinalysis, glucose, HbA1c.
Send all lab work to reference labs; no in-office testing.

decisions.title: Spirometry System
decisions.description: Pulmonary function testing device for asthma and COPD screening.

options.description: Portable, PC-based spirometer with ultrasonic flow sensor and EHR integration.
Handheld diagnostic spirometer with Bluetooth connectivity.
Refer PFTs to pulmonology or diagnostic center.

decisions.title: Diagnostic Imaging
decisions.description: In-office X-ray or ultrasound capabilities.

options.description: In-office digital radiography with PACS integration for musculoskeletal and chest imaging.
Point-of-care ultrasound (Butterfly iQ+ or GE Vscan) for guided exams.
Refer all imaging to radiology centers and hospitals.

decisions.title: Clinical Photography
decisions.description: Standardized photo documentation for wound care, dermatology, and progress tracking.

options.description: Professional clinical photography system with standardized lighting and EHR integration.
HIPAA-compliant photo capture on mobile devices with Modica or similar apps.
No standardized clinical photography program.

decisions.title: Connected Scales & Body Composition
decisions.description: Digital scales with EHR integration for automatic weight and BMI capture.

options.option_name: Health o meter Professional Connected Scale
options.description: Professional-grade connected scale with EMR integration and height rod.
Basic digital scale with manual weight entry into EHR.
Advanced body composition analyzer providing detailed metrics beyond weight.
risks: May be overkill for primary care

categories.name: Telephony & Communications
categories.description: Phone systems, after-hours answering, paging, fax, and unified communications.

decisions.title: Phone System (VoIP)
decisions.description: Select a business phone system for the practice.

options.description: Cloud UCaaS platform with voice, video, messaging, and contact center.
Cloud communications with voice, video, chat, and analytics.
Self-hosted or cloud PBX with softphone, mobile app, and web client.
Business VoIP with CRM integration, voicemail-to-email, and auto-attendant.

decisions.title: After-Hours Answering
decisions.description: Handle patient calls outside business hours.

options.description: HIPAA-compliant medical answering service with nurse triage protocols.
Configure VoIP auto-attendant to route urgent calls to on-call provider cell phone.
AI phone agent that handles after-hours calls, triages urgency, and routes appropriately.

decisions.title: Paging & Clinical Alerts
decisions.description: In-office paging or clinical alert system for staff communication.

options.description: HIPAA-compliant clinical communication platform replacing pagers.
Use Microsoft Teams with healthcare-specific features for staff communication.
Use regular phone intercom and non-clinical texting.

decisions.title: Fax Solution
decisions.description: HIPAA-compliant fax for referrals, records requests, and lab communications.

options.description: HIPAA-compliant cloud fax with EHR integration and Direct messaging.
Integrated fax within RingCentral phone system.
Traditional fax machine or multifunction printer with fax capability.

decisions.title: Unified Communications
decisions.description: Integrated platform for voice, video, messaging, and collaboration.

options.description: Microsoft 365 with Teams Phone System add-on for full UCaaS.
Zoom unified platform with meetings, phone, chat, and whiteboard.
Use separate tools: phone system + video conferencing + messaging app.

categories.name: Security & Compliance
categories.description: HIPAA security controls, endpoint protection, identity management, backup and disaster recovery.

decisions.title: Endpoint Protection (EDR/XDR)
decisions.description: Antivirus, anti-malware, and endpoint detection and response for all workstations.

options.description: Cloud-native EDR with AI-powered threat detection and response.
Autonomous AI-powered endpoint protection with rollback capabilities.
Microsoft-integrated endpoint protection included with M365 Business Premium.
Multi-layered endpoint security with risk analytics and hardening.

decisions.title: Email Security & Encryption
decisions.description: Secure email with encryption, phishing protection, and HIPAA compliance.

options.description: M365 email with Purview message encryption, DLP, and Advanced Threat Protection.
Google Workspace email with Virtru end-to-end encryption add-on.
HIPAA-compliant email encryption that works seamlessly with any email provider.

decisions.title: Identity & Access Management
decisions.description: User authentication, MFA, SSO, and access control.

options.description: Cloud identity with SSO, MFA, and conditional access policies.
Duo MFA for multi-factor authentication across applications and VPN.
Cloud identity platform with SSO, MFA, and lifecycle management.
risks: May be overkill for small practice

options.description: Use built-in MFA from Google Workspace or M365 without additional identity platform.

decisions.title: Physical Security
decisions.description: Physical access controls, cameras, and security for server rooms and medication storage.

options.description: Cloud-managed cameras, access control, and environmental sensors.
Ring cameras for surveillance with Kisi cloud access control for doors.
Physical key locks with standalone security cameras and local DVR.

decisions.title: Backup & Disaster Recovery
decisions.description: Data backup strategy and disaster recovery planning for business continuity.

options.description: Datto SIRIS or ALTO for image-based backup with instant local and cloud virtualization.
Veeam Backup & Replication with Wasabi or AWS S3 cloud storage.
If fully cloud/SaaS-based, use SaaS backup for M365/Google and rely on vendor uptime.
Integrated backup and cybersecurity platform with ransomware protection.

decisions.title: SIEM & Audit Logging
decisions.description: Security information and event management for HIPAA audit trail requirements.

options.description: Cloud SIEM designed for SMBs with automated detection and response.
Managed detection and response with 24/7 SOC monitoring.
Rely on built-in EHR audit logging and basic Windows Event Logs.

categories.name: Data & Analytics
categories.description: Quality reporting, clinical dashboards, population health, financial analytics, and registry reporting.

decisions.title: Quality Measure Reporting
decisions.description: MIPS/MACRA quality measure tracking and CMS reporting.

options.description: Use built-in quality reporting dashboards in your EHR for MIPS submission.
Qualified registry for MIPS reporting with measure optimization and support.
Data reporting and visualization for clinical quality measures and UDS.

decisions.title: Clinical Dashboards
decisions.description: Visual dashboards for clinical performance, care gaps, and practice metrics.

options.description: Leverage reporting and dashboard tools included in your EHR.
Power BI dashboards connected to EHR data extracts and practice databases.
Tableau analytics platform with healthcare-specific accelerators.

decisions.title: Population Health Management
decisions.description: Tools for managing patient populations, identifying care gaps, and improving outcomes.

options.description: Unified patient data platform with care management and population health analytics.
Population health analytics with data aggregation from multiple sources.
Use EHR built-in population health features for basic panel management.
Manage population health manually through EHR reports and registries.

decisions.title: Financial Analytics & RCM
decisions.description: Revenue cycle analytics, denial management, and financial performance dashboards.

options.description: Use financial reports built into your practice management system.
Automated payment variance detection and underpayment recovery.
Full revenue cycle management outsourcing to a specialized firm.

decisions.title: Data Warehouse
decisions.description: Centralized data repository for cross-system analytics and reporting.

options.description: Modern cloud data warehouse for aggregating EHR, PM, and operational data.
Run reports directly from each system without centralizing data.

decisions.title: Registry & Public Health Reporting
decisions.description: Immunization registry, syndromic surveillance, and public health reporting.

options.description: Use EHR built-in interfaces for immunization registries and public health reporting.
Immunization information system connectivity and registry services.
Manually enter data into state immunization registries and public health portals.

categories.name: Patient-Facing Technology
categories.description: Self-service kiosks, digital intake, patient Wi-Fi, payments, communications, telehealth, RPM, and digital signage.

decisions.title: Patient Check-In Kiosk
decisions.description: Self-service check-in for patient arrival, insurance verification, and copay collection.

options.description: Patient intake platform with check-in tablets, insurance capture, and payment collection.
Patient-facing kiosks with eligibility verification and self-registration.
Consumer iPads with check-in app from your EHR vendor in kiosk mode.
Keep traditional front desk check-in with staff handling all registration.

decisions.title: Digital Intake Forms
decisions.description: Electronic patient intake forms, consent, and pre-visit questionnaires.

options.description: Digital forms sent to patients before visits via text/email with EHR integration.
HIPAA-compliant digital forms with customizable templates and basic EHR integration.
risks: Manual EHR entry may be needed

options.description: Use built-in patient forms from your EHR/portal.

decisions.title: Patient Wi-Fi
decisions.description: Separate guest Wi-Fi network for patients in waiting and exam areas.

options.description: Separate VLAN/SSID on practice APs with bandwidth limits and content filtering.
Guest Wi-Fi with captive portal requiring agreement to terms of use.
Do not offer patient Wi-Fi; patients use cellular data.

decisions.title: Payment Processing
decisions.description: Patient payment collection including copays, balances, and payment plans.

options.description: Healthcare-specific payment platform with contactless, online, and text-to-pay options.
Square payment processing with HIPAA-compliant invoicing and card readers.
Use payment processing built into your practice management system.

decisions.title: Patient Communication Platform
decisions.description: Automated appointment reminders, recall, surveys, and marketing communications.

options.description: Patient engagement platform with reminders, waitlist, surveys, and referral management.
HIPAA-compliant messaging, appointment reminders, and digital intake.
Use built-in EHR appointment reminders and recall features.

decisions.title: Telehealth Platform
decisions.description: Video visit platform for virtual patient encounters.

options.description: Simple, browser-based telehealth platform with free and paid tiers.
Use telehealth module from your EHR vendor (e.g., athena Telehealth, eCW TeleMed).
Zoom with HIPAA BAA and healthcare-specific features.

decisions.title: Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)
decisions.description: Connected devices for monitoring chronic conditions between visits.

options.description: RPM platform with devices, software, and billing support for chronic care.
RPM platform focused on primary care with cellular-connected devices and monitoring.
Defer RPM program until practice is established and has chronic care patient panel.

decisions.title: Digital Signage
decisions.description: Waiting room and lobby digital displays for patient education and practice information.

options.description: Healthcare-specific digital signage with educational content and pharma-sponsored content.
Consumer smart TVs with health education channels or practice-branded slides.
Use traditional printed materials and posters in waiting areas.

categories.name: Administrative Systems
categories.description: HR, payroll, learning management, contracts, credentialing, time tracking, and scheduling systems.

decisions.title: HR & Payroll System
decisions.description: Human resources management, payroll processing, and benefits administration.

options.description: All-in-one HR platform with payroll, benefits, and compliance for small businesses.
ADP payroll and HR solution for small practices with tax filing and compliance.
Payroll, HR, and benefits with dedicated payroll specialist support.
Have your CPA or bookkeeper handle payroll as part of accounting services.

decisions.title: Learning Management System
decisions.description: Staff training, HIPAA education, OSHA compliance, and continuing education tracking.

options.description: Healthcare-specific compliance training, credentialing, and policy management.
Security awareness training with phishing simulation focused on cybersecurity.
Track training completion with spreadsheets and manual documentation.

decisions.title: Contract Management
decisions.description: Tracking payer contracts, vendor agreements, and lease terms.

options.description: AI-powered contract lifecycle management platform.

options.option_name: Google Drive / SharePoint with Tracking Sheet
options.description: Store contracts in cloud storage with a spreadsheet tracking key dates and terms.
Document automation with e-signatures, templates, and contract tracking.

decisions.title: Provider Credentialing
decisions.description: Manage provider credentialing, re-credentialing, and payer enrollment.

options.description: Automated provider credentialing and payer enrollment platform.
Maintain CAQH profiles and handle credentialing paperwork in-house.
Hire a credentialing specialist or service to manage all provider enrollment.

decisions.title: Time & Attendance
decisions.description: Employee time tracking, scheduling, and attendance management.

options.description: Workforce management with scheduling, time tracking, and compliance.
Free time clock and scheduling for small teams with paid upgrades.
Use time tracking features built into your payroll provider.

decisions.title: Staff & Provider Scheduling
decisions.description: Provider schedule templates, staff scheduling, and resource management.

options.description: Provider scheduling and on-call management for healthcare organizations.
risks: May be overkill for small practice

options.description: Use the scheduling features in your EHR or practice management system.
Create provider schedules using spreadsheets or paper-based methods.

categories.name: Integration & Interoperability
categories.description: Interface engines, health information exchange, FHIR APIs, referral management, and third-party integrations.

decisions.title: Interface Engine
decisions.description: HL7/FHIR integration engine for connecting clinical systems.

options.description: Open-source integration engine for HL7, FHIR, and custom healthcare interfaces.
Enterprise healthcare integration platform with visual interface design.
Cloud-based API integration platform connecting healthcare applications.

options.option_name: Skip - Rely on EHR Built-in Integrations
options.description: Use only the integrations built into your EHR system.

decisions.title: Health Information Exchange (HIE)
decisions.description: Connect to regional or national health information exchange networks.

options.description: Join your regional HIE for query-based and push-based clinical data exchange.
National interoperability frameworks typically accessed through your EHR vendor.
Use Direct messaging protocol for point-to-point secure clinical messaging.

decisions.title: FHIR API Strategy
decisions.description: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources API for modern data exchange.

options.description: Leverage FHIR APIs provided by your EHR vendor per ONC requirements.
FHIR-based data aggregation platform for patient records from multiple sources.

options.option_name: Skip Advanced FHIR - Use EHR Defaults
options.description: Rely on basic EHR FHIR compliance without additional investment.

decisions.title: Referral Management
decisions.description: Electronic referral tracking, specialist communication, and referral loop closure.

options.description: Referral management platform with provider directory, e-referrals, and tracking.
Use built-in referral management features in your EHR.
Traditional fax-based referral process with manual tracking.

decisions.title: Care Coordination Platform
decisions.description: Tools for care team communication, care plans, and transitions of care.

options.description: Care coordination platform for transitions, post-acute management, and network management.
Use EHR care plan features with secure messaging for team coordination.
Handle care coordination through phone, fax, and EHR documentation.

decisions.title: Third-Party Integration Strategy
decisions.description: Overall approach to connecting ancillary systems and services.

options.description: Use integration platforms like Zapier or Make for connecting non-clinical SaaS tools.
Build custom integrations with APIs as needed for specific workflows.
Rely only on integrations pre-built by your software vendors.

categories.name: Remote Access & Continuity
categories.description: VPN, virtual desktop, downtime procedures, and cloud vs. on-premises strategy.

decisions.title: VPN Solution
decisions.description: Secure remote access VPN for staff and providers working outside the office.

options.description: Enterprise SSL VPN through Cisco or Meraki firewall with client-based VPN.
Modern, lightweight VPN protocol with excellent performance and simple configuration.
Zero-config mesh VPN using WireGuard with identity-based access.
If all applications are cloud/SaaS, VPN may not be necessary.
risks: Not suitable if any on-prem resources

decisions.title: Virtual Desktop Infrastructure
decisions.description: VDI or DaaS for centralized desktop management and remote access.

options.description: Microsoft Azure-hosted virtual desktops with Windows 10/11 multi-session.
AWS-hosted persistent virtual desktops with hourly or monthly billing.
Use VPN or cloud apps directly without virtual desktop infrastructure.

decisions.title: Downtime Procedures
decisions.description: Paper-based and alternative workflow procedures for system outages.

options.description: Pre-printed downtime forms, procedures binder, and offline medication reference.
Cached/offline-capable tablets with patient schedules and key reference data.
Minimal paper forms for capturing visits during EHR downtime.

decisions.title: Cloud vs. On-Premises Strategy
decisions.description: Overall technology hosting strategy for the practice.

options.description: Prioritize SaaS and cloud-hosted solutions for all IT systems.
Cloud EHR and SaaS with on-premises networking, printing, and local backup.
Host critical systems on-premises with cloud only for specific SaaS tools.

categories.name: AI & Emerging Technology
categories.description: Ambient documentation, AI coding/billing, predictive analytics, conversational AI, and AI governance.

decisions.title: Ambient Clinical Documentation
decisions.description: AI-powered ambient listening for automatic clinical note generation during patient encounters.

options.description: Microsoft/Nuance ambient AI that auto-generates clinical notes from patient conversations.
AI-powered ambient documentation with structured note generation and EHR integration.
Voice-powered AI assistant for clinical documentation and EHR navigation.
Continue with traditional typing, dictation, or scribes for clinical documentation.

decisions.title: AI-Assisted Coding & Billing
decisions.description: AI tools for automated medical coding, charge capture, and billing optimization.

options.description: Autonomous medical coding engine that reads clinical documentation and assigns codes.
AI medical coding assistant that suggests codes from clinical notes.
Use trained medical coders or provider self-coding without AI assistance.

decisions.title: Predictive Analytics
decisions.description: AI/ML models for predicting patient risk, no-shows, and operational patterns.

options.description: Clinical AI for patient risk prediction and actionable intervention recommendations.
Use predictive features built into your EHR (e.g., Epic Deterioration Index, risk scores).
Defer advanced predictive analytics until practice is larger and has more data.

decisions.title: Conversational AI & Chatbots
decisions.description: AI-powered patient-facing chatbots for triage, scheduling, and FAQ.

options.description: Conversational AI for healthcare with phone, chat, and SMS patient interactions.
Simple FAQ chatbot on practice website for common patient questions.
Handle all patient interactions through traditional phone and portal messaging.

decisions.title: AI Governance & Policy
decisions.description: Framework for evaluating, deploying, and monitoring AI tools in clinical practice.

options.description: Establish AI governance committee, evaluation criteria, bias monitoring, and patient consent policies.
Basic AI use policy covering approved tools, documentation requirements, and human oversight.
Address AI governance as AI tools are adopted on a case-by-case basis.
